visual: NEW data context: "Points Scored" Vertical Data Table: Round 1: 22, Round 2: 15, Round 3: 8, Round 4: 31
guide: "Here's data from a game. Four rounds of points: 22, 15, 8, 31."
guide_2: "Check the ones place digits: 2, 5, 8, 1. Do they all end in 0 or 5?"
guide_3: "22... no. 15... yes! 8... no. 31... no. Three of them don't so they aren't multiples of 5. Which scale will show ALL values exactly?"
prompt: "Which scale will show ALL values exactly?"
on_correct: Visual - Scale of 2 "Points Scored" vertical bar graph shown. "Scale of 2. All of the bars fit well on the graph and their exact values are clear."
remediation_light: "Look at the last digits: 2, 5, 8, 1. Most aren't 0 or 5. Which scale works for any number?"
visual_transition: Side-by-side comparison (same height bars, only axis markings changed): Scale of 2 "Points Scored" vertical bar graph Scale of 1 "Points Scored" vertical bar graph
guide_transition: "Scale of 1 also shows the exact values, but it would require a lot of marks on the axis to get to the greatest value of 31. Let's consider when we might choose a scale of 1."
